description: Developer tools and infrastructure companies selling to engineering teams of 5-100+ developers. Characterized by bottom-up adoption patterns, technical evaluation processes, and deals typically under $5K with 4-7 week sales cycles. Buyers are often individual developers or engineering managers who champion tools internally.

pain_points.pain: Build times exceeding 15 minutes killing developer productivity
pain_points.context: Long CI/CD pipelines create context switching and slow feedback loops

pain_points.pain: Production incidents taking too long to resolve (high MTTR)
pain_points.context: Poor observability and debugging tools extend outage duration

pain_points.pain: Scaling engineering team from 15 to 40+ developers
pain_points.context: Processes and tools that worked for small teams break down at scale

pain_points.pain: Build vs buy decisions consuming engineering resources
pain_points.context: Teams often default to building internal tools instead of buying proven solutions
Moving from monolith to microservices creates new tooling and observability needs

pain_points.pain: Developer experience degradation as codebase grows
pain_points.context: Slow local development, flaky tests, and poor documentation reduce velocity

buying_triggers: Production incident causing significant customer impact
Developer attrition linked to poor tooling
budget_cycle: Engineering budgets typically set annually but with discretionary spending authority for tools under $5K. Bottom-up adoption often starts with team credit cards before formal procurement.
regulatory_concerns: Data handling for production debugging tools

terminology.definition: Developer Experience - the overall experience developers have when using tools, APIs, and platforms
terminology.usage_context: Quality metric. 'Our DX is suffering because of slow build times.'

terminology.definition: Developer Experience Index - quantitative measurement of developer experience
terminology.usage_context: Benchmarking. 'We track DXI quarterly to measure improvements.'

terminology.definition: Continuous Integration/Continuous Deployment - automated build, test, and deployment pipelines
terminology.usage_context: Core infrastructure. 'Our CI/CD pipeline takes 20 minutes to complete.'

terminology.definition: Ability to understand system state through logs, metrics, and traces
terminology.usage_context: Monitoring capability. 'We need better observability to debug production issues.'

terminology.definition: Service Level Objective/Indicator/Agreement - targets, measurements, and commitments for service reliability
terminology.usage_context: Reliability metrics. 'Our SLO is 99.9% uptime measured by SLI of error rate.'

terminology.definition: Discipline of building and maintaining internal developer platforms to improve productivity
terminology.usage_context: Team function. 'Our platform engineering team maintains the internal developer portal.'

terminology.definition: Internal Developer Platform/Portal - self-service platform for developers to provision resources
terminology.usage_context: Infrastructure. 'The IDP lets developers spin up new services without tickets.'

terminology.definition: Infrastructure as Code - managing infrastructure through code rather than manual processes
terminology.usage_context: Practice. 'All our infrastructure is defined in Terraform as IaC.'

terminology.definition: Using Git as the source of truth for declarative infrastructure and applications
terminology.usage_context: Deployment practice. 'We use GitOps so all changes go through pull requests.'

terminology.definition: Time required to compile and test code changes
terminology.usage_context: Productivity metric. 'Our build time has crept up to 18 minutes.'

terminology.definition: Time from pull request creation to merge
terminology.usage_context: Velocity metric. 'Our average PR cycle time is 2 days.'

terminology.definition: DevOps Research and Assessment metrics - deployment frequency, lead time, MTTR, change failure rate
terminology.usage_context: Industry benchmarks. 'We track DORA metrics to compare against elite performers.'

terminology.definition: Mean Time To Recovery - average time to restore service after an incident
terminology.usage_context: Reliability metric. 'Our MTTR is currently 45 minutes which is too high.'

terminology.definition: Architecture pattern where applications are composed of small, independent services
terminology.usage_context: Architecture. 'We migrated from monolith to microservices last year.'

terminology.definition: Container orchestration platform for automating deployment, scaling, and management
terminology.usage_context: Infrastructure. 'We run everything on Kubernetes in AWS.'

common_objections.underlying_concern: Engineering teams often believe internal solutions will be better tailored to their needs and avoid vendor dependency
common_objections.effective_response_approach: Calculate total cost of ownership including maintenance, opportunity cost of engineers not building product features, and ongoing iteration. Reference industry data on build vs buy outcomes.

common_objections.objection: We don't have time to evaluate and implement new tools right now
common_objections.underlying_concern: Team is under delivery pressure and adding new tools feels like a distraction
common_objections.effective_response_approach: Acknowledge the time pressure. Propose minimal viable adoption that solves the most acute pain. Show time-to-value data from similar teams. Offer to handle setup.

common_objections.objection: This doesn't fit with our current stack/architecture
common_objections.underlying_concern: Fear of adding complexity or forcing architectural changes
common_objections.effective_response_approach: Deep dive on their specific stack. Show integration patterns with their technologies. Provide architecture guidance for adoption path.

common_objections.objection: Migrating our existing setup is too risky
common_objections.underlying_concern: Production systems are fragile and any change could cause outages
common_objections.effective_response_approach: Propose incremental migration strategy. Offer to run parallel for a period. Share migration playbooks from similar customers. Provide migration support.

common_objections.objection: I like it but I can't get the team to adopt it
common_objections.underlying_concern: Developers are creatures of habit and resist changing workflows
common_objections.effective_response_approach: Provide adoption playbook. Identify early adopters on the team. Show developer-friendly features. Offer team training or champions program.

common_objections.objection: We need SSO/SOC 2 and you don't have it
common_objections.underlying_concern: Security team blocks tools without proper enterprise security features
common_objections.effective_response_approach: Share roadmap for security features. Offer interim security measures. Connect with security team directly. Consider design partner arrangement for enterprise features.

common_objections.objection: The pricing doesn't scale well for our team size
common_objections.underlying_concern: Per-seat pricing becomes expensive as team grows
common_objections.effective_response_approach: Explore alternative pricing models. Show value at scale. Offer volume discounts or flat-tier pricing. Compare to total cost of alternatives including internal tools.

typical_incumbent_solutions: Open source tools requiring self-hosting and maintenance
Built-in features from cloud providers (AWS, GCP, Azure)
switching_barriers: Institutional knowledge embedded in current setup
Time investment to learn new tools

scenario_templates.template: {{role}} at a {{company_size}} company is frustrated with {{pain_point}}. Their team of {{team_size}} developers currently uses {{current_solution}} but it's causing {{specific_issue}}.

A {{company_type}} is scaling their engineering team from {{current_size}} to {{target_size}} developers over the next {{timeline}}. They need to upgrade their {{tooling_area}} before the current approach breaks down.
{{role}} discovered your tool and loves it, but needs to convince their {{stakeholder}} to approve the purchase. The main concerns are {{concerns}} and budget is {{budget}}.
After a production incident that took {{incident_duration}} to resolve, {{role}} at {{company_type}} is looking for better {{tooling_area}} tooling. They have executive support but need to show ROI.

{{company_type}} with {{team_size}} developers is migrating from monolith to microservices. {{role}} is evaluating {{tooling_area}} solutions and has narrowed it down to your product and {{competitor}}.
